text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: freestatemoney R package loading parsing campaign finance data Maryland. package provide functions access analyze Maryland’s campaign finance reporting data.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: follows standard R package conventions: R/ - R source code package functions man/ - Documentation files (generated roxygen2 comments) tests/testthat/ - Unit tests using testthat framework data/ - Data files included package () vignettes/ - Long-form documentation tutorials DESCRIPTION - Package metadata dependencies NAMESPACE - Function exports (generated roxygen2)

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Install development dependencies install.packages("devtools") install.packages("roxygen2") install.packages("testthat")  # Load package for development devtools::load_all()  # Build documentation from roxygen2 comments devtools::document()  # Install package locally devtools::install()  # Build package tarball devtools::build()

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a specific test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-filename.R")  # Check package for CRAN compliance devtools::check()

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Check code style lintr::lint_package()  # Run R CMD check (comprehensive package validation) devtools::check()

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Function Development: Create functions R/ directory roxygen2 documentation comments Documentation: Use roxygen2 format (#') exported function @export tag Testing: Write tests tests/testthat/ following naming convention test-*.R Updates: Run devtools::document() changing roxygen2 comments update NAMESPACE man/ files

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Every exported function :
code: #' Brief one-line description #' #' More detailed description if needed. #' #' @param param_name Description of parameter #' @return Description of return value #' @export #' @examples #' \dontrun{ #' example_code() #' }

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Maryland campaign finance data available Maryland State Board Elections https://campaignfinance.maryland.gov/public/cf/downloads data provided three main datasets:

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Committee registration metadata including: - Committee identification (Filing Entity ID, name, type) - Officers (treasurer/authorized agent, chairperson/principal officer addresses) - Committee contact information (address, phone, email, website, social media) - Registration dates (submission, approval, dissolution) - Candidate information (name, DOB, address, office sought, jurisdiction, party) - Committee-specific fields PACs, ballot issues, IE/EC committees, public financing Key fields: Filing Entity ID (unique identifier), Committee Type (Candidate/PAC/Party/etc), Election

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Individual contribution loan transactions including: - Committee information (Filing Entity ID, name, type) - Contributor details (type, name, company, address) - Transaction details (type, date, amount, payment method) - Fund type (electoral, administrative, compliance) - Public funding eligibility (qualifying contributions) - Aggregates download date Key fields: Filing Entity ID, Transaction Date, Transaction Amount, Transaction Type, Contributor Type, Fund Type Special handling: Lump sum contributions blank contributor details

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Spending transactions including: - Committee information (Filing Entity ID, name, type) - Payee details (type, name/company, address) - Vendor information (optional secondary recipient) - Transaction details (date, amount, category, purpose, fund type) - -kind contribution tracking - Independent Expenditure specific fields (candidate/ballot issue, office sought, position, amount applied) Key fields: Filing Entity ID, Transaction Type, Transaction Date, Transaction Amount, Category, Purpose, Fund Type Transaction Types: Expenditure, Outstanding Obligation, Independent Expenditure (IE), Electioneering Communication (EC)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: implementing data loading functions: Field Naming: Convert field positions (, B, C…) readable column names using snake_case Date Handling: Parse date fields (Transaction Date, Registration dates) Date objects Amount Handling: Parse amount fields numeric, handling currency formatting Nullable Fields: Many fields nullable - handle NAs appropriately Relationships: Filing Entity ID links three datasets IDs character (preserve leading zeros ) Amounts numeric Dates Date Categorical fields (Committee Type, Transaction Type, etc.) factors character

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Functions support: - Loading complete datasets filtering date ranges - Aggregating contributions contributor, committee, time period - Tracking expenditures category, purpose, payee - Linking contributions expenditures via Filing Entity ID - Analyzing Independent Expenditures candidate/issue - Committee lookups metadata queries

title: CLAUDE.md
text: MIT License - Copyright (c) 2026 NewsAppsUMD

title: How to Download Maryland Campaign Finance Data
text: package can download bulk data directly Maryland SBE’s CRIS API — endpoint website’s download buttons use: Notes: File size: full-cycle contribution expenditure files can run hundreds megabytes. Passing year keeps downloads manageable. Available years: SBE typically offers recent several filing years (currently back 2019). Older years return “data” error. Committees available one complete file; year ignored.
code: library(freestatemoney)  # Current-cycle committee list committees_path <- md_download("committees")  # Contributions or expenditures for a specific filing year contributions_path <- md_download("contributions", year = 2025) expenditures_path <- md_download("expenditures", year = 2025)  # Feed the paths straight into the loaders committees <- md_committees(committees_path)

title: How to Download Maryland Campaign Finance Data
text: Visit download page: https://campaignfinance.maryland.gov/public/cf/downloads Select data type: Committees: Registration metadata committees Contributions Loans: contribution transactions Expenditures: spending transactions (includes IE/EC) Select time period: current cycle specific filing year Click download button save CSV file Pass file path md_committees(), md_contributions(), md_expenditures()

title: How to Download Maryland Campaign Finance Data
text: file begins metadata line like Committee Download 06/14/2026 01:00 actual header. loaders skip automatically expose timestamp attr(df, "download_date"). Values leading zeros (zip codes) wrapped Excel ="21228"; loaders unwrap . Amounts dollar-formatted ($3,000.00); loaders parse numeric.

title: How to Download Maryland Campaign Finance Data
text: Maryland State Board Elections regenerates bulk files periodically (roughly daily) new reports filed. download_date attribute tells exactly file extracted.

title: Quick Start Guide
text: Install package: Load library: Get data md_download() (download CSVs manually https://campaignfinance.maryland.gov/public/cf/downloads — see DOWNLOAD_INSTRUCTIONS.md)
code: # Install from GitHub devtools::install_github("NewsAppsUMD/freestatemoney") library(freestatemoney) library(dplyr)  # recommended for data manipulation

title: Quick Start Guide
code: # Current-cycle committee list; year-specific transaction files committees_file <- md_download("committees") contributions_file <- md_download("contributions", year = 2025) expenditures_file <- md_download("expenditures", year = 2025)

title: Quick Start Guide
text: Returns information registered campaign finance committees including: - Committee names types - Officers treasurers - Candidates (applicable) - Registration dates - Contact information
code: committees <- md_committees(committees_file)

title: Quick Start Guide
text: Returns contribution loan transactions including: - gave money (contributor details) - much (transaction amounts) - (transaction dates) - (committee information) - Payment method
code: contributions <- md_contributions(contributions_file)

title: Quick Start Guide
text: Returns spending transactions including: - Regular expenditures - Outstanding obligations - Independent expenditures - Electioneering communications
code: expenditures <- md_expenditures(expenditures_file)

title: Quick Start Guide
code: # Search by name committees %>%   filter(grepl("Moore", committee_name, ignore.case = TRUE))  # Get candidate committees only committees %>%   filter(committee_type == "Candidate")

title: Quick Start Guide
code: # Load the data first committees <- md_committees(md_download("committees")) contributions <- md_contributions(md_download("contributions", year = 2025))  # Get Filing Entity ID from committees table committee_id <- "YOUR_COMMITTEE_ID"  # Get all contributions to that committee committee_contributions <- contributions %>%   filter(filing_entity_id == committee_id) %>%   arrange(desc(transaction_amount))

title: Quick Start Guide
code: large_contributions <- contributions %>%   filter(transaction_amount >= 1000) %>%   select(     committee_name,     contributor_last_name,     contributor_first_name,     transaction_amount,     transaction_date   ) %>%   arrange(desc(transaction_amount))

title: Quick Start Guide
code: expenditures %>%   group_by(category) %>%   summarize(     total = sum(transaction_amount, na.rm = TRUE),     count = n()   ) %>%   arrange(desc(total))

title: Quick Start Guide
code: independent_exp <- expenditures %>%   filter(transaction_type == "Independent Expenditure") %>%   select(     committee_name,     candidate_ballot_issue,     position,     transaction_amount,     transaction_date,     purpose   )

title: Quick Start Guide
code: # Add committee details to contributions contributions_detailed <- contributions %>%   left_join(     committees %>% select(filing_entity_id, committee_type, office_sought),     by = "filing_entity_id"   )  # Calculate total raised and spent per committee committee_finances <- committees %>%   left_join(     contributions %>%       group_by(filing_entity_id) %>%       summarize(raised = sum(transaction_amount, na.rm = TRUE)),     by = "filing_entity_id"   ) %>%   left_join(     expenditures %>%       group_by(filing_entity_id) %>%       summarize(spent = sum(transaction_amount, na.rm = TRUE)),     by = "filing_entity_id"   ) %>%   mutate(balance = raised - spent)

title: Quick Start Guide
text: filing_entity_id: Unique committee ID (links three datasets)

title: Quick Start Guide
text: transaction_date: transaction occurred registration_submission_date: committee registered registration_approval_date: committee approved

title: Quick Start Guide
text: transaction_amount: Dollar amount transaction amounts numeric (character strings)

title: Quick Start Guide
text: committee_type: Candidate, PAC, Party, Public financing, etc. contributor_type: Individual, Business, PAC, etc. transaction_type: Contribution, Loan, Expenditure, IE, EC, etc. fund_type: Electoral, Administrative, Compliance

title: Quick Start Guide
text: Use clean_names = TRUE (default) consistent snake_case column names Filter date focus specific time periods Use filing_entity_id link data across three datasets Check NAs - many fields nullable Aggregate amounts using sum() na.rm = TRUE

title: Quick Start Guide
text: See inst/examples/basic_analysis.R detailed examples Read full documentation: ?md_committees, ?md_contributions, ?md_expenditures Check tidyverse documentation: https://www.tidyverse.org/

title: Analyzing Maryland Campaign Finance Data
text: md_download() fetches bulk CSVs directly SBE’s API — data download page https://campaignfinance.maryland.gov/public/cf/downloads serves. Full-cycle transaction files can run hundreds megabytes, pass year limit downloads single filing year: vignette can run anywhere, uses small samples real CRIS data ship package:
code: committees <- md_committees(md_download("committees")) contributions <- md_contributions(md_download("contributions", year = 2025)) expenditures <- md_expenditures(md_download("expenditures", year = 2025))  # Or all three at once: md <- md_load_all(year = 2025) sample_file <- function(name) {   system.file("extdata", name, package = "freestatemoney") }  committees <- md_committees(sample_file("committees_sample.csv")) contributions <- md_contributions(sample_file("contributions_sample.csv")) expenditures <- md_expenditures(sample_file("expenditures_sample.csv"))

what: What the loaders handle for you
title: Analyzing Maryland Campaign Finance Data
text: Raw CRIS files several quirks loaders take care : metadata line like Committee Download 06/14/2026 01:00 precedes header. skipped, timestamp kept: Amounts arrive dollar-formatted ($3,000.00) parsed numeric; dates (MM/DD/YYYY) become Date objects: Zip codes arrive wrapped Excel (="21228") unwrapped; files read actual encoding (typically Windows-1252, UTF-8). loaders also refuse load wrong dataset — passing contributions file md_committees() produces error naming function wanted.
code: attr(committees, "download_date") #> [1] "2026-06-14 01:00:00 UTC" contributions %>%   select(transaction_date, transaction_amount) %>%   head(3) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   transaction_date transaction_amount #>   <date>                        <dbl> #> 1 2024-11-20                      3.6 #> 2 2024-06-25                     10   #> 3 2025-05-16                   3000

title: Analyzing Maryland Campaign Finance Data
code: committees %>%   count(committee_type, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   committee_type          n #>   <chr>               <int> #> 1 Candidate Committee     8  committees %>%   filter(committee_type == "Candidate Committee") %>%   select(committee_name, candidate_last_name, office_sought, party_affiliation) %>%   head(5) #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   committee_name             candidate_last_name office_sought party_affiliation #>   <chr>                      <chr>               <chr>         <chr>             #> 1 Guthrie, Dion F. Citizens… GUTHRIE             County Counc… Democrat          #> 2 Rosapepe, Jim Friends Of   ROSAPEPE            State Senator Democrat          #> 3 Washington, Mary M. Citiz… WASHINGTON          Board of Edu… Other             #> 4 Kearney, Reginald Friends… KEARNEY             Judge of the… Democrat          #> 5 Lee, Cereta A. Friends Of  LEE                 Register of … Democrat

title: Analyzing Maryland Campaign Finance Data
text: md_top_contributors() aggregates contributor — organizations company name, individuals “Last, First” — ranks total:  full year data, call surfaces largest donors state.
code: top <- md_top_contributors(contributions, n = 10) top #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   contributor                 contributor_type             total n_contributions #>   <chr>                       <chr>                        <dbl>           <int> #> 1 Friends of Jessica Feldmark Business/Group/Organization 3000                 1 #> 2 Feldmark, Joshua            Individual                    20.5               2 #> 3 Maroshek, Agnes             Individual                    10                 1 #> 4 GRUNDY, JOSEPH              Individual                     8.1               3 #> 5 HEATH, JAN                  Individual                     3.6               1 library(ggplot2)  top %>%   mutate(contributor = reorder(contributor, total)) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = total, y = contributor)) +   geom_col(fill = "#4666DE", width = 0.7) +   scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::dollar) +   labs(     title = "Total contributions by contributor",     subtitle = "Package sample data",     x = NULL, y = NULL   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())

title: Analyzing Maryland Campaign Finance Data
text: Independent expenditures electioneering communications — spending candidates rather — get summary. CRIS labels combined transaction types, md_independent_expenditures() matches pattern:
code: expenditures %>%   group_by(category) %>%   summarize(     total = sum(transaction_amount, na.rm = TRUE),     n = n()   ) %>%   arrange(desc(total)) #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   category                          total     n #>   <chr>                             <dbl> <int> #> 1 Salaries and Other Compensation 17680       4 #> 2 Field Expenses                   8294.      3 #> 3 Printing and Campaign Materials  1320       1 #> 4 Other Expenses                     42.7     2 md_independent_expenditures(expenditures) #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   committee_name     candidate_ballot_issue  position total_spent n_expenditures #>   <chr>              <chr>                   <chr>          <dbl>          <int> #> 1 Casa in Action PAC Adams-Stafford, Shayla… Support         7930              2

title: Analyzing Maryland Campaign Finance Data
text: md_committee_summary() joins three datasets filing_entity_id totals committee’s fundraising spending. Outstanding obligations (unpaid debts) excluded spending, committees transactions get zeros: Note sample files cover different committees, totals mostly zero; real full-year downloads every active committee represented. net_raised reflects transactions data loaded — committee’s official cash balance.
code: md_committee_summary(committees, contributions, expenditures) %>%   head(5) #> # A tibble: 5 × 8 #>   filing_entity_id committee_name    committee_type total_raised n_contributions #>   <chr>            <chr>             <chr>                 <dbl>           <int> #> 1 1000012          Guthrie, Dion F.… Candidate Com…            0               0 #> 2 1000076          Rosapepe, Jim Fr… Candidate Com…            0               0 #> 3 1000450          Washington, Mary… Candidate Com…            0               0 #> 4 1000561          Kearney, Reginal… Candidate Com…            0               0 #> 5 1000598          Lee, Cereta A. F… Candidate Com…            0               0 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: total_spent <dbl>, n_expenditures <int>, net_raised <dbl>

title: Analyzing Maryland Campaign Finance Data
text: filing_entity_id links three datasets, join works:
code: contributions %>%   left_join(     committees %>% select(filing_entity_id, office_sought, party_affiliation),     by = "filing_entity_id"   ) %>%   group_by(party_affiliation) %>%   summarize(total_raised = sum(transaction_amount, na.rm = TRUE))

title: Analyzing Maryland Campaign Finance Data
text: Lump-sum contributions blank contributor details; dropped md_top_contributors() present raw data. Aggregate Download Date gives contributor’s running total committee file’s extraction date. Contributor names reported: person may appear multiple spellings. SBE regenerates bulk files roughly daily; check attr(df, "download_date") file’s extraction time.

title: Working with Large Files via DuckDB
text: md_db() opens (creates) database file; md_db_load() downloads dataset ingests . Different years accumulate; re-ingesting year replaces , refreshes idempotent: database file persists, later sessions just reopen — re-downloading: vignette build throwaway database sample data shipped package:
code: con <- md_db("maryland.duckdb")  md_db_load(con, "committees") md_db_load(con, "contributions", year = 2024) md_db_load(con, "contributions", year = 2025) md_db_load(con, "expenditures", year = 2025) con <- md_db("maryland.duckdb") md_db_tables(con) sample_file <- function(name) {   system.file("extdata", name, package = "freestatemoney") }  con <- md_db(tempfile(fileext = ".duckdb")) #> duckdb: caching downloaded extensions in the package library: #> ℹ /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/duckdb/extensions #> ℹ This is removed when the package is re-installed; see `?duckdb_storage` to choose a different location. md_db_load(con, "committees", file = sample_file("committees_sample.csv")) md_db_load(con, "contributions", file = sample_file("contributions_sample.csv")) md_db_tables(con) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   dataset       filing_year n_rows download_date       ingested_at         #>   <chr>         <chr>        <dbl> <dttm>              <dttm>              #> 1 committees    current          8 2026-06-14 01:00:00 2026-07-10 11:14:56 #> 2 contributions current          8 2026-07-06 01:00:00 2026-07-10 11:14:56

title: Working with Large Files via DuckDB
text: dplyr::tbl() gives lazy table: filters, joins, aggregations compile SQL run inside DuckDB. Data enters R collect(): Joins across datasets work way, entirely inside database (two sample files happen cover different committees, join empty ; real full-year downloads every committee matches): filing_year column added ingest lets multi-year analyses group year:
code: contributions <- tbl(con, "contributions")  contributions |>   group_by(contributor_type) |>   summarize(     total = sum(transaction_amount, na.rm = TRUE),     n = n()   ) |>   arrange(desc(total)) |>   collect() #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   contributor_type             total     n #>   <chr>                        <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 Business/Group/Organization 3000       1 #> 2 Individual                    42.2     7 tbl(con, "contributions") |>   inner_join(     tbl(con, "committees") |> select(filing_entity_id, office_sought),     by = "filing_entity_id"   ) |>   count(office_sought) |>   collect() #> # A tibble: 0 × 2 #> # ℹ 2 variables: office_sought <chr>, n <dbl> tbl(con, "contributions") |>   group_by(filing_year) |>   summarize(total_raised = sum(transaction_amount, na.rm = TRUE)) |>   collect()

title: Working with Large Files via DuckDB
text: Ingestion applies treatment md_contributions() friends: metadata line skipped, names cleaned snake_case, dates dollar amounts typed, Excel armor stripped, CRIS export defects (bare line breaks inside fields, Windows-1252 bytes, truncated -long records) repaired identically. query database returns values tibble :
code: tbl(con, "contributions") |>   select(transaction_date, transaction_amount, contributor_zip_code) |>   head(3) |>   collect() #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   transaction_date transaction_amount contributor_zip_code #>   <date>                        <dbl> <chr>                #> 1 2024-11-20                      3.6 21228                #> 2 2024-06-25                     10   21044                #> 3 2025-05-16                   3000   21044

title: Working with Large Files via DuckDB
text: .duckdb file remains disk next time (delete like file ’re done ).
code: DBI::dbDisconnect(con)

what: Maryland campaign finance data in R
title: Maryland campaign finance data in R
text: freestatemoney R package downloading, loading, parsing campaign finance data Maryland. works data Maryland State Board Elections Campaign Reporting Information System (CRIS), returning tidy data frames ready analysis.

title: Maryland campaign finance data in R
text: can install development version freestatemoney GitHub :
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("NewsAppsUMD/freestatemoney")

title: Maryland campaign finance data in R
text: md_download() fetches bulk data directly SBE’s CRIS API — data download page https://campaignfinance.maryland.gov/public/cf/downloads serves: grab everything : can also download CSV files manually SBE website pass paths loader functions. See DOWNLOAD_INSTRUCTIONS.md details.
code: library(freestatemoney)  # Current-cycle committee list committees <- md_committees(md_download("committees"))  # Contributions for a single filing year (full-cycle files can be very large) contributions <- md_contributions(md_download("contributions", year = 2025))  # Expenditures for a single filing year expenditures <- md_expenditures(md_download("expenditures", year = 2025)) md <- md_load_all(year = 2025)

title: Maryland campaign finance data in R
text: data comes Maryland State Board Elections Campaign Reporting Information System (CRIS): https://campaignfinance.maryland.gov/public/cf/downloads package provides access three main datasets: Committees: Registration metadata campaign finance committees Contributions Loans: Individual contribution loan transactions Expenditures: Spending transactions including expenditures, outstanding obligations, independent expenditures

title: Maryland campaign finance data in R
code: library(freestatemoney)  # Load committee registration data from a downloaded file committees <- md_committees("CommitteeList.csv")  # View structure head(committees)

title: Maryland campaign finance data in R
code: library(freestatemoney) library(dplyr)  # Load contributions from a downloaded file contributions <- md_contributions("ContributionsList.csv")  # Find large contributions large_contributions <- contributions %>%   filter(transaction_amount >= 500) %>%   arrange(desc(transaction_amount))

title: Maryland campaign finance data in R
code: library(freestatemoney) library(dplyr)  # Load expenditures from a downloaded file expenditures <- md_expenditures("ExpendituresList.csv")  # Summarize spending by category spending_summary <- expenditures %>%   group_by(category) %>%   summarize(     total_amount = sum(transaction_amount, na.rm = TRUE),     transaction_count = n()   ) %>%   arrange(desc(total_amount))  # Filter for Independent Expenditures independent_expenditures <- expenditures %>%   filter(transaction_type == "Independent Expenditure")

title: Maryland campaign finance data in R
code: md <- md_load_all(year = 2025)  # Fundraising and spending totals per committee md_committee_summary(md$committees, md$contributions, md$expenditures)  # Largest donors md_top_contributors(md$contributions, n = 25)  # Independent expenditure / electioneering communication spending md_independent_expenditures(md$expenditures)

title: Maryland campaign finance data in R
text: Full-cycle transaction files run hundreds megabytes. md_db() family (requires suggested duckdb DBI packages) ingests bulk data persistent DuckDB database , queries lazily — results come R: See “Working Large Files via DuckDB” vignette full workflow.
code: con <- md_db("maryland.duckdb") md_db_load(con, "contributions", year = 2024) md_db_load(con, "contributions", year = 2025)  dplyr::tbl(con, "contributions") |>   dplyr::filter(transaction_amount >= 1000) |>   dplyr::collect()  DBI::dbDisconnect(con)

title: Maryland campaign finance data in R
code: library(freestatemoney) library(dplyr)  committees <- md_committees(md_download("committees")) contributions <- md_contributions(md_download("contributions", year = 2025))  # Join to add committee details to contributions contributions_with_details <- contributions %>%   left_join(     committees %>% select(filing_entity_id, election),     by = "filing_entity_id"   )  # Analyze contributions by committee type by_committee_type <- contributions_with_details %>%   group_by(committee_type) %>%   summarize(     total_raised = sum(transaction_amount, na.rm = TRUE),     avg_contribution = mean(transaction_amount, na.rm = TRUE),     num_contributions = n()   )

title: Maryland campaign finance data in R
text: Direct downloads: md_download() pulls bulk data straight CRIS API Tidy Data: functions return tibbles clean column names Type Safety: Dates parsed Date objects, dollar-formatted amounts numeric CRIS quirks handled: metadata line header skipped (exposed download_date attribute), Excel-armored values like ="21228" unwrapped Wrong-file detection: Passing contributions file md_committees() produces helpful error instead broken data frame

title: Maryland campaign finance data in R
text: default, functions convert column names snake_case (e.g., filing_entity_id, transaction_amount). can disable setting clean_names = FALSE; date amount columns still parsed:
code: # With clean names (default) committees <- md_committees("path/to/file.csv", clean_names = TRUE)  # With original column names committees_raw <- md_committees("path/to/file.csv", clean_names = FALSE)

title: Maryland campaign finance data in R
text: Filing Entity ID unique identifier links three datasets fields may nullable/NA depending transaction committee type Lump sum contributions blank contributor details Independent Expenditure transactions additional fields candidate/ballot issue information Date format source files MM/DD/YYYY; amounts dollar-formatted (e.g. $3,000.00) raw file begins “… Download ” metadata line; parsed timestamp available attr(df, "download_date")

what: Find bare line breaks inside fields — bare_lf_positions
title: Find bare line breaks inside fields — bare_lf_positions
text: CRIS records terminated CRLF; bare LF (preceded CR) field content — typically multi-line address — breaks row every CSV parser. Returns positions LF bytes `chunk`. Quote state deliberately tracked: CRIS data contains stray literal quotes unquoted fields, make quote-parity unreliable, legal quoted line breaks CRLF file use CRLF (leaves alone).

title: Find bare line breaks inside fields — bare_lf_positions
code: bare_lf_positions(chunk, prev_byte)

title: Find bare line breaks inside fields — bare_lf_positions
text: chunk Raw vector file bytes prev_byte Last byte preceding chunk

title: Find bare line breaks inside fields — bare_lf_positions
text: Integer positions within `chunk`

what: Summarize fundraising and spending by committee — md_committee_summary
title: Summarize fundraising and spending by committee — md_committee_summary
text: Joins three CRIS datasets `filing_entity_id` totals committee raised spent. Outstanding obligations (unpaid debts) excluded spending totals. Committees transactions get zeros rather NAs.

title: Summarize fundraising and spending by committee — md_committee_summary
code: md_committee_summary(committees, contributions, expenditures)

title: Summarize fundraising and spending by committee — md_committee_summary
text: committees Tibble [md_committees()] contributions Tibble [md_contributions()] expenditures Tibble [md_expenditures()]

title: Summarize fundraising and spending by committee — md_committee_summary
text: tibble one row per committee: `filing_entity_id`,   `committee_name`, `committee_type`, `total_raised`, `n_contributions`,   `total_spent`, `n_expenditures`, `net_raised`, sorted   `total_raised` descending.

title: Summarize fundraising and spending by committee — md_committee_summary
text: Note `net_raised` reflects transactions data loaded - committee's official cash balance, also depends prior balances non-itemized activity.

title: Summarize fundraising and spending by committee — md_committee_summary
code: sample_file <- function(name) {   system.file("extdata", name, package = "freestatemoney") } committees <- md_committees(sample_file("committees_sample.csv")) contributions <- md_contributions(sample_file("contributions_sample.csv")) expenditures <- md_expenditures(sample_file("expenditures_sample.csv"))  md_committee_summary(committees, contributions, expenditures) #> # A tibble: 8 × 8 #>   filing_entity_id committee_name    committee_type total_raised n_contributions #>   <chr>            <chr>             <chr>                 <dbl>           <int> #> 1 1000012          Guthrie, Dion F.… Candidate Com…            0               0 #> 2 1000076          Rosapepe, Jim Fr… Candidate Com…            0               0 #> 3 1000450          Washington, Mary… Candidate Com…            0               0 #> 4 1000561          Kearney, Reginal… Candidate Com…            0               0 #> 5 1000598          Lee, Cereta A. F… Candidate Com…            0               0 #> 6 1000626          Franchot, Peter … Candidate Com…            0               0 #> 7 1000660          Pipkin, E.J. Fri… Candidate Com…            0               0 #> 8 1000674          Madaleno, Richar… Candidate Com…            0               0 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: total_spent <dbl>, n_expenditures <int>, net_raised <dbl>

title: Load Maryland Committee Data — md_committees
text: Parses committee registration data Maryland State Board Elections. Returns tidy data frame committee metadata including registration information, officers, contact details.

title: Load Maryland Committee Data — md_committees
code: md_committees(file_path, clean_names = TRUE)

title: Load Maryland Committee Data — md_committees
text: file_path Character string. Path local committee CSV file downloaded Maryland SBE. parameter required. clean_names Logical. TRUE (default), converts column names snake_case. Date columns parsed either way.

title: Load Maryland Committee Data — md_committees
text: tibble committee data including: filing_entity_id: Unique committee identifier committee_name: Full committee name committee_type: Type (Candidate, PAC, Party, etc.) election: Assigned election(s) registration_submission_date: Date registration submitted registration_approval_date: Date registration approved registration_dissolved_date: Date committee dissolved (applicable) many additional fields officers, candidates, contact   information. tibble carries `download_date` attribute   file's "" timestamp.

title: Load Maryland Committee Data — md_committees
text: Download committee CSV [md_download("committees")][md_download], manually Maryland SBE website https://campaignfinance.maryland.gov/public/cf/downloads raw file metadata line ("Committee Download ...") header; skipped automatically timestamp stored `download_date` attribute result. Values wrapped Excel `="21085"` (zip codes) unwrapped plain strings.

title: Load Maryland Committee Data — md_committees
code: # Sample data included with the package committees <- md_committees(   system.file("extdata", "committees_sample.csv", package = "freestatemoney") ) head(committees) #> # A tibble: 6 × 62 #>   filing_entity_id committee_name abbreviated_committe…¹ committee_type election #>   <chr>            <chr>          <chr>                  <chr>          <chr>    #> 1 1000012          Guthrie, Dion… NA                     Candidate Com… Guberna… #> 2 1000076          Rosapepe, Jim… NA                     Candidate Com… Guberna… #> 3 1000450          Washington, M… NA                     Candidate Com… Preside… #> 4 1000561          Kearney, Regi… NA                     Candidate Com… Guberna… #> 5 1000598          Lee, Cereta A… NA                     Candidate Com… Guberna… #> 6 1000626          Franchot, Pet… NA                     Candidate Com… Preside… #> # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​abbreviated_committee_name #> # ℹ 57 more variables: treasurer_authorized_agent_name <chr>, #> #   treasurer_authorized_agent_public_address1 <chr>, #> #   treasurer_authorized_agent_address2 <chr>, #> #   treasurer_authorized_agent_city <chr>, #> #   treasurer_authorized_agent_state <chr>, #> #   treasurer_authorized_agent_zip_code <chr>, …  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Download and load in one go committees <- md_committees(md_download("committees"))  # With original column names (not snake_case) committees <- md_committees("path/to/committees.csv", clean_names = FALSE) } # }

what: Load Maryland Contributions and Loans Data — md_contributions
title: Load Maryland Contributions and Loans Data — md_contributions
text: Parses contribution loan transaction data Maryland State Board Elections. Returns tidy data frame contributor details, transaction amounts, dates.

title: Load Maryland Contributions and Loans Data — md_contributions
code: md_contributions(file_path, clean_names = TRUE)

title: Load Maryland Contributions and Loans Data — md_contributions
text: file_path Character string. Path local contributions CSV file downloaded Maryland SBE. parameter required. clean_names Logical. TRUE (default), converts column names snake_case. Date amount columns parsed either way.

title: Load Maryland Contributions and Loans Data — md_contributions
text: tibble contribution loan data including: filing_entity_id: Committee identifier (links committees) committee_name: Name receiving committee committee_type: Type committee contributor_type: Type contributor (Individual, Business, PAC, etc.) transaction_type: Type transaction (contribution, loan, etc.) transaction_date: Date transaction (Date) transaction_amount: Amount transaction (numeric) payment_type: Payment method (Cash, Check, Credit Card, etc.) fund_type: Fund type (electoral, administrative, compliance) additional fields contributor address, public funding   eligibility, etc. tibble carries `download_date` attribute   file's "" timestamp.

title: Load Maryland Contributions and Loans Data — md_contributions
text: Download contributions CSV [md_download("contributions")][md_download], manually Maryland SBE website https://campaignfinance.maryland.gov/public/cf/downloads raw file metadata line ("Contributions Loan Download ...") header; skipped automatically timestamp stored `download_date` attribute result. Dollar-formatted amounts (`$3,000.00`) parsed numeric, values wrapped Excel `="21228"` (zip codes) unwrapped plain strings.

title: Load Maryland Contributions and Loans Data — md_contributions
code: # Sample data included with the package contributions <- md_contributions(   system.file("extdata", "contributions_sample.csv", package = "freestatemoney") ) head(contributions) #> # A tibble: 6 × 28 #>   filing_entity_id committee_name          abbreviated_committe…¹ committee_type #>   <chr>            <chr>                   <chr>                  <chr>          #> 1 3007773          MSEA's Fund For Childr… NA                     PAC            #> 2 1012725          Feldmark, Jessica Frie… NA                     Candidate      #> 3 8015370          International Brotherh… IBEW PAC               Out-of-State … #> 4 1012725          Feldmark, Jessica Frie… NA                     Candidate      #> 5 3007773          MSEA's Fund For Childr… NA                     PAC            #> 6 3007773          MSEA's Fund For Childr… NA                     PAC            #> # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​abbreviated_committee_name #> # ℹ 24 more variables: contributor_type <chr>, contributor_company_name <chr>, #> #   contributor_last_name <chr>, contributor_first_name <chr>, #> #   contributor_middle_name <chr>, contributor_mailing_address1 <chr>, #> #   contributor_mailing_address2 <chr>, contributor_city <chr>, #> #   contributor_state <chr>, contributor_zip_code <chr>, #> #   contributor_county_of_residence <chr>, transaction_type <chr>, …  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Download a single filing year and load it contributions <- md_contributions(md_download("contributions", year = 2025))  # Filter for large contributions library(dplyr) large_contributions <- md_contributions("path/to/contributions.csv") %>%   filter(transaction_amount >= 500) } # }

what: Open a DuckDB database for Maryland campaign finance data — md_db
title: Open a DuckDB database for Maryland campaign finance data — md_db
text: Creates (reopens) persistent DuckDB database hold CRIS bulk data. Use files large work comfortably memory — full-cycle contribution files run hundreds megabytes, multi-year analyses multiply . Ingest data [md_db_load()], query lazily `dplyr::tbl()`; results ever come R.

title: Open a DuckDB database for Maryland campaign finance data — md_db
code: md_db(path = "freestatemoney.duckdb")

title: Open a DuckDB database for Maryland campaign finance data — md_db
text: path Path DuckDB database file (default `"freestatemoney.duckdb"` working directory). Created exist; reopened previously ingested data intact otherwise.

title: Open a DuckDB database for Maryland campaign finance data — md_db
text: DBI connection database. Close   `DBI::dbDisconnect()` finished.

title: Open a DuckDB database for Maryland campaign finance data — md_db
text: Requires suggested packages duckdb DBI: `install.packages(c("duckdb", "DBI"))`.

title: Open a DuckDB database for Maryland campaign finance data — md_db
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ con <- md_db("maryland.duckdb") md_db_load(con, "contributions", year = 2024) md_db_load(con, "contributions", year = 2025)  dplyr::tbl(con, "contributions") |>   dplyr::filter(transaction_amount >= 1000) |>   dplyr::collect()  DBI::dbDisconnect(con) } # }

what: Ingest a Maryland dataset into a DuckDB database — md_db_load
title: Ingest a Maryland dataset into a DuckDB database — md_db_load
text: Loads CRIS bulk CSV table database opened [md_db()], applying parsing -memory loaders — metadata line skipped, column names cleaned snake_case, dates dollar amounts typed, Excel armor (`="21228"`) stripped — inside DuckDB, file never fit R's memory.

title: Ingest a Maryland dataset into a DuckDB database — md_db_load
code: md_db_load(   con,   type = c("committees", "contributions", "expenditures"),   year = NULL,   file = NULL,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Ingest a Maryland dataset into a DuckDB database — md_db_load
text: con Connection [md_db()] type dataset: "committees", "contributions", "expenditures". Used table name. year Optional four-digit filing year, passed [md_download()] downloading used `filing_year` tag. file Optional path already-downloaded CSV. NULL, data downloaded via [md_download()] temporary file. quiet Logical. FALSE (default), shows download progress bar.

title: Ingest a Maryland dataset into a DuckDB database — md_db_load
text: lazy table (`dplyr::tbl(con, type)`), invisibly.

title: Ingest a Maryland dataset into a DuckDB database — md_db_load
text: ingest tagged `filing_year` column (`"current"` `year` NULL). Ingesting dataset year replaces year's rows, refreshes idempotent; different years accumulate. Provenance (row counts, file's "" timestamp) recorded visible via [md_db_tables()].

title: Ingest a Maryland dataset into a DuckDB database — md_db_load
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ con <- md_db() md_db_load(con, "expenditures", year = 2025) md_db_load(con, "expenditures", file = "already_downloaded.csv") } # }

what: List what has been ingested into a Maryland DuckDB database — md_db_tables
title: List what has been ingested into a Maryland DuckDB database — md_db_tables
text: List ingested Maryland DuckDB database

title: List what has been ingested into a Maryland DuckDB database — md_db_tables
code: md_db_tables(con)

title: List what has been ingested into a Maryland DuckDB database — md_db_tables
text: con Connection [md_db()]

title: List what has been ingested into a Maryland DuckDB database — md_db_tables
text: tibble one row per ingested dataset/year: `dataset`,   `filing_year`, `n_rows`, `download_date` (file's "" timestamp),   `ingested_at`.

title: List what has been ingested into a Maryland DuckDB database — md_db_tables
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ con <- md_db() md_db_load(con, "contributions", year = 2025) md_db_tables(con) } # }

what: Download Maryland Campaign Finance Data — md_download
title: Download Maryland Campaign Finance Data — md_download
text: Downloads bulk data CSV directly Maryland State Board Elections Campaign Reporting Information System (CRIS) API - endpoint download page https://campaignfinance.maryland.gov/public/cf/downloads uses.

title: Download Maryland Campaign Finance Data — md_download
code: md_download(   type = c("committees", "contributions", "expenditures"),   year = NULL,   path = NULL,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Download Maryland Campaign Finance Data — md_download
text: type dataset download: "committees", "contributions", "expenditures". year Optional four-digit filing year (e.g. 2025). NULL (default), downloads current-cycle file. SBE typically offers recent several years. path save CSV. Defaults file named dataset (year, given) current working directory. quiet Logical. FALSE (default), shows download progress bar.

title: Download Maryland Campaign Finance Data — md_download
text: path downloaded CSV file, invisibly suitable   passing straight [md_committees()], [md_contributions()],   [md_expenditures()].

title: Download Maryland Campaign Finance Data — md_download
text: contributions expenditures files full cycle can large (hundreds megabytes); passing `year` limits download single filing year. Committee data available complete file, `year` ignored `type = "committees"`.

title: Download Maryland Campaign Finance Data — md_download
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Current-cycle committee list committees <- md_committees(md_download("committees"))  # Contributions for a single filing year contributions <- md_contributions(md_download("contributions", year = 2025)) } # }

what: Load Maryland Expenditures, Outstanding Obligations, and IE/EC Data — md_expenditures
title: Load Maryland Expenditures, Outstanding Obligations, and IE/EC Data — md_expenditures
text: Parses expenditure transaction data Maryland State Board Elections. Returns tidy data frame payee details, transaction amounts, purposes, categories. Includes regular expenditures, outstanding obligations, independent expenditures (IE), electioneering communications (EC).

title: Load Maryland Expenditures, Outstanding Obligations, and IE/EC Data — md_expenditures
code: md_expenditures(file_path, clean_names = TRUE)

title: Load Maryland Expenditures, Outstanding Obligations, and IE/EC Data — md_expenditures
text: file_path Character string. Path local expenditures CSV file downloaded Maryland SBE. parameter required. clean_names Logical. TRUE (default), converts column names snake_case. Date amount columns parsed either way.

title: Load Maryland Expenditures, Outstanding Obligations, and IE/EC Data — md_expenditures
text: tibble expenditure data including: filing_entity_id: Committee identifier (links committees) committee_name: Name spending committee committee_type: Type committee transaction_type: Type (Expenditure, Outstanding Obligation, IE, EC) payee_type: Type payee (Business, Self, Candidate, PAC, etc.) transaction_date: Date transaction (Date) transaction_amount: Amount transaction (numeric) category: Expenditure category purpose: Purpose expenditure fund_type: Fund type (electoral, administrative, compliance) additional fields vendor information, IE-specific fields, etc.   tibble carries `download_date` attribute file's ""   timestamp.

title: Load Maryland Expenditures, Outstanding Obligations, and IE/EC Data — md_expenditures
text: Download expenditures CSV [md_download("expenditures")][md_download], manually Maryland SBE website https://campaignfinance.maryland.gov/public/cf/downloads raw file metadata line ("Expenditure Download ...") header; skipped automatically timestamp stored `download_date` attribute result. Dollar-formatted amounts (`$1,320.00`) parsed numeric, values wrapped Excel `="20814"` (zip codes) unwrapped plain strings.

title: Load Maryland Expenditures, Outstanding Obligations, and IE/EC Data — md_expenditures
code: # Sample data included with the package expenditures <- md_expenditures(   system.file("extdata", "expenditures_sample.csv", package = "freestatemoney") ) head(expenditures) #> # A tibble: 6 × 37 #>   filing_entity_id committee_name          abbreviated_committe…¹ committee_type #>   <chr>            <chr>                   <chr>                  <chr>          #> 1 1014521          Bienenfeld, Paula Frie… NA                     Candidate      #> 2 13014810         Casa in Action PAC      NA                     Super PAC      #> 3 13014810         Casa in Action PAC      NA                     Super PAC      #> 4 13014810         Casa in Action PAC      NA                     Super PAC      #> 5 13014810         Casa in Action PAC      NA                     Super PAC      #> 6 1009852          Bhandari, Harry (H.B.)… NA                     Candidate      #> # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​abbreviated_committee_name #> # ℹ 33 more variables: payee_type <chr>, payee_company_name <chr>, #> #   payee_last_name <chr>, payee_first_name <chr>, payee_middle_name <chr>, #> #   payee_country <chr>, payee_mailing_address1 <chr>, #> #   payee_mailing_address2 <chr>, payee_city <chr>, payee_state <chr>, #> #   payee_zip_code <chr>, vendor_type <chr>, vendor_name <chr>, #> #   vendor_country <chr>, vendor_mailing_address1 <chr>, …  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Download a single filing year and load it expenditures <- md_expenditures(md_download("expenditures", year = 2025))  # Analyze spending by category library(dplyr) by_category <- md_expenditures("path/to/expenditures.csv") %>%   group_by(category) %>%   summarize(total = sum(transaction_amount, na.rm = TRUE))  # Filter for Independent Expenditures only ie_only <- md_expenditures("path/to/expenditures.csv") %>%   filter(transaction_type == "Independent Expenditure") } # }

what: Summarize independent expenditures and electioneering communications — md_independent_expenditures
title: Summarize independent expenditures and electioneering communications — md_independent_expenditures
text: Filters expenditure data independent expenditure (IE) electioneering communication (EC) transactions totals spending committee, target candidate ballot issue, position.

title: Summarize independent expenditures and electioneering communications — md_independent_expenditures
code: md_independent_expenditures(expenditures)

title: Summarize independent expenditures and electioneering communications — md_independent_expenditures
text: expenditures Tibble [md_expenditures()]

title: Summarize independent expenditures and electioneering communications — md_independent_expenditures
text: tibble `committee_name`, `candidate_ballot_issue`,   `position`, `total_spent`, `n_expenditures`, sorted `total_spent`   descending.

title: Summarize independent expenditures and electioneering communications — md_independent_expenditures
text: Note: CRIS reports transaction types like "Independent Expenditure / Electioneering Communication", matching pattern, exact equality.

title: Summarize independent expenditures and electioneering communications — md_independent_expenditures
code: expenditures <- md_expenditures(   system.file("extdata", "expenditures_sample.csv", package = "freestatemoney") ) md_independent_expenditures(expenditures) #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   committee_name     candidate_ballot_issue  position total_spent n_expenditures #>   <chr>              <chr>                   <chr>          <dbl>          <int> #> 1 Casa in Action PAC Adams-Stafford, Shayla… Support         7930              2

what: Download and load all three Maryland datasets at once — md_load_all
title: Download and load all three Maryland datasets at once — md_load_all
text: Convenience wrapper downloads committees, contributions, expenditures via [md_download()] loads parser. `year` applies contributions expenditures; committee data available one complete file.

title: Download and load all three Maryland datasets at once — md_load_all
code: md_load_all(year = NULL, dir = tempdir(), quiet = FALSE)

title: Download and load all three Maryland datasets at once — md_load_all
text: year Optional four-digit filing year passed [md_download()] transaction datasets. NULL, downloads current-cycle files, can large. dir Directory save downloaded CSVs (default: temporary directory). quiet Logical. FALSE (default), shows download progress bars.

title: Download and load all three Maryland datasets at once — md_load_all
text: named list `committees`, `contributions`,   `expenditures` tibbles, ready joining `filing_entity_id`.

title: Download and load all three Maryland datasets at once — md_load_all
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ md <- md_load_all(year = 2025) summary <- md_committee_summary(md$committees, md$contributions, md$expenditures) } # }

what: Repair CRIS CSV defects before parsing — prepare_md_csv
title: Repair CRIS CSV defects before parsing — prepare_md_csv
text: Streams file chunks looking two defects real CRIS exports : bare LF line breaks inside unquoted fields (split rows), Windows-1252 bytes (appear megabytes , past encoding-sniffing windows, break UTF-8 parsers). Clean files returned untouched; defective ones rewritten temporary file bare LFs replaced spaces text transcoded UTF-8. CRLF line breaks inside quoted fields legal CSV left alone.

title: Repair CRIS CSV defects before parsing — prepare_md_csv
code: prepare_md_csv(file_path)

title: Repair CRIS CSV defects before parsing — prepare_md_csv
text: file_path Path downloaded CSV file

title: Repair CRIS CSV defects before parsing — prepare_md_csv
text: list `path` (file parse), `repaired` (number   line breaks fixed), `reencoded` (logical)

what: Read a Maryland CRIS bulk download CSV — read_md_csv
title: Read a Maryland CRIS bulk download CSV — read_md_csv
text: Shared reader three CRIS datasets. Validates file expected dataset, skips "... Download " metadata line, repairs CRIS export defects (see [prepare_md_csv()]), reads columns character, strips Excel formula armor, converts date amount columns, optionally cleans column names.

title: Read a Maryland CRIS bulk download CSV — read_md_csv
code: read_md_csv(file_path, dataset, clean_names = TRUE)

title: Read a Maryland CRIS bulk download CSV — read_md_csv
text: file_path Path downloaded CSV file dataset One "committees", "contributions", "expenditures" clean_names Logical; convert names snake_case

title: Read a Maryland CRIS bulk download CSV — read_md_csv
text: tibble `download_date` attribute (POSIXct, NA   metadata line absent)

title: Strip Excel formula armor from a character vector — strip_excel_armor

what: Utility functions for freestatemoney package — utils
title: Utility functions for freestatemoney package — utils
text: Internal utility functions used across package.

title: freestatemoney 0.2.0
text: New DuckDB-backed workflow large multi-year files: md_db() opens persistent database, md_db_load() downloads ingests dataset/year (idempotently — re-ingesting year replaces ), md_db_tables() reports loaded, provenance. Query lazily dplyr::tbl(). Requires suggested duckdb DBI packages. See “Working Large Files via DuckDB” vignette. Loaders (DuckDB ingest) now repair defects found real CRIS exports: bare line breaks inside unquoted fields (split rows every CSV parser), Windows-1252 bytes appearing beyond encoding-sniffing windows, records missing extra fields. paths produce identical results, verified full-year files. New md_download() downloads bulk data directly Maryland SBE CRIS API — manual clicking downloads page. Supports current cycle specific filing year. md_load_all() downloads loads three datasets one call. New analysis helpers: md_committee_summary() totals fundraising spending per committee, md_top_contributors() ranks donors, md_independent_expenditures() summarizes IE/EC spending target position. New vignette, “Analyzing Maryland Campaign Finance Data”, runnable sample CRIS data now included inst/extdata. pkgdown site configuration deploy workflow. Loaders now detect file wrong dataset passed (e.g. contributions CSV given md_committees()) error pointer right function. “… Download ” timestamp raw file exposed download_date attribute returned tibble.

title: freestatemoney 0.2.0
text: Loaders now actually parse real CRIS files. Previously metadata line precedes header treated header, column type specifications never matched Title Case headers, dollar-formatted amounts read numeric. Dates now returned Date amounts numeric, whether clean_names used. Excel formula armor values leading zeros (e.g. zip codes stored ="21228") stripped. Files read detected encoding (CRIS files typically Windows-1252, UTF-8).

title: freestatemoney 0.2.0
text: Placeholder tests replaced real suite driven fixture files taken actual CRIS downloads. Removed unused dependencies (dplyr, tibble); httr now used md_download(). Documentation (man/) now generated; added GitHub Actions R CMD check.

title: freestatemoney 0.1.0
text: Initial version.
